config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society’s interests matter a lot, but they should not automatically come before individual rights, because that can easily be abused by governments or majorities. In my view, a healthy society protects personal freedom, fair treatment, and due process first, then balances them with the common good. From my perspective, the line should be drawn carefully: society can limit rights only when there is a clear and necessary reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu justru menjadi dasar agar mayoritas atau pemerintah tidak semena-mena, dan sejarah kami penuh contoh ketika “demi kepentingan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan batas yang ketat, alasan yang jelas, dan perlindungan proses hukum, bukan dengan mengorbankan kebebasan pribadi begitu saja. Kalau hak individu mudah dikalahkan, masyarakat mungkin tampak tertib, tetapi sebenarnya jadi rapuh dan berbahaya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to “the public interest” can be used to silence criticism, excuse unequal treatment, or pressure people to conform, so I am wary of giving society automatic supremacy. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every case, because public safety, public health, and social order sometimes require real limits. The key point is that any limit must be narrow, necessary, and accountable, otherwise “society” becomes a convenient excuse for abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai aturan umum, karena di Amerika kami punya pengalaman panjang bahwa alasan “demi kepentingan masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan terhadap individu. Hak individu harus menjadi titik awal, bukan sesuatu yang mudah dikalahkan oleh mayoritas atau pemerintah, meskipun saya juga mengakui ada keadaan tertentu seperti keselamatan publik dan kesehatan umum yang memang bisa membenarkan pembatasan terbatas. Yang penting, pembatasan itu harus sempit, perlu, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan sekadar karena masyarakat ingin hasil yang lebih nyaman atau seragam. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” berubah jadi slogan yang terdengar mulia tetapi berbahaya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement as a general principle, because in Indonesia I have seen how “society” can be used as a moral shield for coercion, conformity, and unequal treatment. That said, I do not treat individual rights as unlimited, because public safety, public health, and basic social order sometimes genuinely require restrictions. What matters is that those limits are narrow, necessary, and accountable, not a blank check for authorities or the majority. If society always comes first, then the individual becomes too easy to sacrifice in the name of a vague common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu harus menjadi dasar utama agar pemerintah atau mayoritas tidak mudah bertindak sewenang-wenang. Pengalaman di AS menunjukkan bahwa dalih “demi kepentingan masyarakat” sering terdengar mulia tetapi bisa dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan, diskriminasi, atau pembatasan kebebasan yang berlebihan. Memang ada situasi seperti keselamatan publik dan kesehatan umum yang dapat membenarkan pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, perlu, dan bisa diuji secara hukum. Kalau tidak, masyarakat mungkin tampak lebih tertib, tetapi sebenarnya justru menjadi lebih rapuh karena kebebasan pribadi terlalu mudah dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
